markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Drift/staleness tracking for compiled wiki pages - the native analog of codewiki's commit-vs-source_files drift check and the S9.1.1 guard that keeps 'query the wiki, not the raw' safe. Every compiled page is stamped with the EventLedger source-version and the exact cited-source set (entity/span/symbol ids + content hashes) it compiled from. A drift checker diffs current authority against stamps and flags exactly which pages are stale and why. Serving a wiki page attaches a staleness verdict fail-closed (reuse the MT-106 StalenessVerdict pattern); the Notes UI receives a machine-readable stale badge contract. Without this MT, MT-241's wiki silently goes stale; they ship together.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Stamps live in PostgreSQL keyed to EventLedger versions; no wall-clock-based staleness heuristics.
Serving a stale page without its verdict is a contract violation (fail-closed).
No false-stale: unchanged sources must not flag pages (negative test required).
scope.expected_behavior: Changing any cited source flags exactly the wiki pages that cited it as STALE with a concrete reason (which source, which version delta); unchanged pages stay FRESH; every served page carries its verdict.
acceptance_criteria: Page stamps record EventLedger version + full cited-source set with content hashes.
Drift check after a real source edit returns exactly the affected page set (proven set equality in test).
Staleness verdict attached on every page-serve path; never silent.
Negative test: recompile with no source change yields zero stale pages.
verification: Real-PG tests: edit-cited-source -> exact stale set; no-change -> no stale; verdict-on-serve.
handoff_requirements: List files changed, migrations, tests with results.
Document the stale-badge contract (fields, values) for the Notes UI + retrieval.

evidence.summary: WikiProjectionDriftAndStaleness: every compiled page is stamped (compile_stamp JSONB) with the EventLedger source version (MAX(event_sequence) watermark) + the EXACT cited-source set (kind/id/content_hash, LM-PWIKI-006; no wall-clock heuristics anywhere). WikiDriftChecker batch-resolves CURRENT hashes (knowledge_sources.content_hash; entity hash = sha256 over kind|key|display|lifecycle|owning-source-hash; loom-block content-field hash; rich-document content_sha256 - the SAME functions used at stamp time, single source of truth) and flags exactly the pages whose cited sources changed with concrete WikiStaleReason {kind, id, stamped vs current hash, source_changed|source_deleted} + ledger version delta. check_workspace persists rebuild_status=stale marks and appends the staleness-verdict receipt (kind wiki_drift_check, LM-PWIKI-012). VERDICT-ON-SERVE fail-closed (LM-PWIKI-008): the api/loom.rs ServedWikiPage wrapper cannot be constructed without a WikiStalenessVerdict; list, single GET, POST compile, POST regenerate, and GET /stale all attach it; evaluation failure fails the serve; unstamped pages verdict 'unstamped' and are NEVER fresh. Ship-together guard live: 0300 CHECK + stamp-required upsert + MT-184 paths stamping.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_wiki/mod.rs - WikiCompileStamp, WikiStaleReason, WikiStalenessVerdict (MT-106 StalenessVerdict pattern: tagged enum + machine label), hash derivations
src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs - ServedWikiPage (serde-flattened page + REQUIRED staleness_verdict), POST /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki/drift-check, verdict attach on every serve path
evidence.stale_badge_contract: staleness_verdict: {"state":"fresh","stamp_ledger_version":int,"current_ledger_version":int} | {"state":"stale","stamp_ledger_version":int,"current_ledger_version":int,"reasons":[{"kind":"source|entity|loom_block|rich_document","id":str,"stamped_content_hash":sha256,"current_content_hash":sha256|null,"change":"source_changed|source_deleted"}]} | {"state":"unstamped"}. unstamped MUST NOT render as fresh. Carried on: GET /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki (each pages[] entry), GET .../wiki/:id, POST .../wiki (compile), POST .../wiki/:id/regenerate, and GET .../wiki/:id/stale ({stale: bool = !fresh, verdict}).

evidence.result: ok. 4 passed; 0 failed (real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL + real Axum loom routes over loopback)
tests: mt242_stamps_record_ledger_version_and_cited_source_hashes - every page stamped; ledger_version in (0, current]; cited hashes are sha256 hex; index page's exact cited set is empty (derives from the catalog)

mt242_source_edit_flags_exactly_the_citing_pages_with_reasons - re-register+re-index REAL staleness.rs with an appended probe symbol; drift stale set == independently derived expected set (set equality both directions); reasons carry the changed source id + stamped vs CURRENT hash + entity-level reasons; unrelated module/entity/decision/index pages stay fresh; stale marks persisted; wiki_drift_check receipt in kernel_event_ledger

mt242_verdict_attached_on_every_serve_path_fail_closed - route-level: list serves EVERY page with a verdict; single GET fresh->stale flip after the real source edit with reasons naming the source; legacy UNSTAMPED row (old upsert path) serves state=unstamped + stale=true (never fresh); POST compile response carries verdict + stamped loom-block citations
mt242_no_change_recompile_yields_zero_stale - zero stale / zero unstamped / all fresh after a no-change recompile (negative test)

evidence.summary: Crash recovery to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after independent adversarial review PASS and fresh current-tree real-PostgreSQL proof for project wiki drift/staleness verdicts. This is Kernel Builder handoff readiness only; validator review remains pending.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.

findings: STALE-SERVE BYPASS (handled+tested): ServedWikiPage makes a verdict-less serve unrepresentable at the type level; every wiki serve route goes through it or computes the verdict explicitly (/stale); verdict evaluation failure fails the request (fail-closed) - no silent-fresh path exists.
UNSTAMPED-AS-FRESH (handled+tested): missing/malformed stamps parse to None -> verdict Unstamped; the /stale route derives stale=true from !is_fresh, so unstamped can never read fresh (LM-PWIKI-008).
FALSE-STALE via wall-clock/touch (handled+tested): hash derivations exclude updated_at and run ids; re-indexing identical content does not flip hashes (no-change negative test green).

HASH-DERIVATION DRIFT between stamp and check (handled by construction): stamp-time and drift-time use the SAME functions (entity_content_hash / loom_block_state_content_hash / source+richdoc hashes); a divergence is impossible without changing both call sites.
VERDICT TRUST (handled): verdicts are computed server-side from authority at serve time; stamps are server-written only - no API surface accepts a stamp.
LEDGER BLOAT via drift receipts (accepted, bounded): the drift receipt embeds per-page stale detail; page list is capped at 2000 by the list query; payloads stay bounded by MAX page count.

STORAGE-LEVEL READS WITHOUT VERDICT (documented): Database::get_loom_wiki_projection is a storage accessor, not a serve path; all HTTP serve paths attach verdicts - consumers must use the API surface (documented in the badge contract).
fixes: No additional code change required beyond the 97c680a3 hardening (title-collision guard, receipt caps, depth cap) recorded under MT-241's review.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-242 requires EventLedger-anchored drift/staleness for compiled project wiki pages: every compiled page stamped with ledger version + exact cited-source set (content hashes, no wall-clock), a drift check that flags exactly the citing pages with concrete reasons, and a fail-closed staleness verdict on every serve path with unstamped-never-fresh. The current tree on feat/WP-KERNEL-009 implements all of this with real PostgreSQL/EventLedger and real Axum-route proof, fully aligned with current Master Spec LM-PWIKI-006/007/008/012.
findings: drift.rs:120-235 (evaluate_stamps) batch-resolves CURRENT content hashes for every cited source kind (source/entity/loom_block/rich_document) and emits per-page Fresh/Stale verdicts; Stale carries concrete WikiStaleReason {kind,id,stamped vs current hash, source_changed|source_deleted}. Matches LM-PWIKI-007.

mod.rs:178-294 defines WikiCompileStamp (ledger_version + sorted/deduped cited_sources), WikiStalenessVerdict tagged enum (fresh/stale/unstamped) with is_fresh()/label(); from_value() returns None for missing/malformed stamps -> Unstamped (never fresh). Matches LM-PWIKI-006/008.

mod.rs:313-365 entity_content_hash and loom_block_state_content_hash deliberately EXCLUDE updated_at and run ids; the SAME functions are reused at stamp time and drift time (drift.rs:156-173), so no false-stale and no stamp/check derivation drift. Satisfies the no-wall-clock and no-false-stale constraints.

api/loom.rs:766-769 ServedWikiPage makes a verdict-less serve unrepresentable; attach_wiki_verdict (828-841) propagates verdict-evaluation errors via map_err(...)? -> HTTP error, never silent fresh. list (966-998), single GET (880-893), compile (850-878), regenerate (919-946), and /stale (895-917) all attach the verdict; /stale derives stale = !is_fresh() so unstamped reads stale. Fail-closed per LM-PWIKI-008.

drift.rs:243-341 check_workspace persists rebuild_status='stale' marks for drifted pages and appends a KnowledgeProjectionRebuilt receipt with payload kind 'wiki_drift_check' (counts + per-page stale/unstamped detail). Satisfies LM-PWIKI-012 staleness-verdict receipt.

migrations/0300_knowledge_wiki_page_compile.sql:38-67 adds compile_stamp JSONB with a CHECK that typed pages MUST have a non-null compile_stamp (page_type IS NULL OR compile_stamp IS NOT NULL) plus structural-validity check and a GIN index on cited_sources -- a database-level ship-together / stamp-required guard.

tests/project_wiki_drift_tests.rs proves behavior against REAL PostgreSQL (PostgresDatabase::connect to managed/operator PG; knowledge_pg_support.rs documents NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback) and REAL Axum routes over a TCP loopback with reqwest. mt242_source_edit_flags_exactly_the_citing_pages_with_reasons re-registers+re-indexes a REAL handshake_core source via the real CodeIndexEngine and asserts set equality both directions against an independently derived expected set (303-306); mt242_no_change_recompile_yields_zero_stale is the negative test (388-402); mt242_verdict_attached_on_every_serve_path_fail_closed covers the fresh->stale flip and the legacy UNSTAMPED row reading state=unstamped/stale=true (628,651).
No deferred-live escapes/placeholders/stubs/todo!/unimplemented! anywhere in drift.rs, mod.rs, or api/loom.rs (grep clean). No forbidden tech (no SQLite, no external search/graph/RAG services).

Minor (non-blocking, attribution only): commit 97c680a3 labeled MT-242 contains only the test file + adversarial hardening (title-collision guard, receipt caps, prosemirror depth cap); the core drift/staleness machinery (drift.rs, ServedWikiPage, stamps) actually landed in the MT-241 commit 9cee7f0a. The required behavior exists and is proven in the current tree on this branch, so this does not affect the verdict.
evidence_checked: git show --stat 97c680a3; git log/-S history for drift.rs, ServedWikiPage, test file

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
